findings.title: CVE-2026-41940 cPanel & WHM Authentication Bypass — 'Sorry' Ransomware Mass-Encrypts 44,000 cPanel Servers, KEV Deadline Today
findings.affected_technology: cPanel & WHM (web hosting control panel) — millions of internet-facing instances; 'Sorry' Go-based Linux ransomware (ChaCha20+RSA-2048) layered on top of the auth bypass
findings.summary: Shadowserver confirms 44,000+ cPanel IPs compromised since CVE-2026-41940 disclosure with attackers now deploying 'Sorry' ransomware on top of the auth-bypass foothold; KEV federal-remediation deadline is today 2026-05-03.

findings.title: BufferZoneCorp Supply-Chain Campaign — 7 Ruby Gems + 9 Go Modules Hijack GitHub Actions, Append SSH Keys, Tamper go.sum
findings.affected_technology: RubyGems ecosystem (knot-* prefix gems) + Go module ecosystem (github.com/BufferZoneCorp/*); CI runners, GitHub Actions, self-hosted runner persistence
findings.summary: Socket disclosed a sleeper-package campaign by BufferZoneCorp/knot-theory: 7 Ruby gems plus 9 Go modules that tamper with go.sum, wrap the real go binary, and append the attacker's SSH key to authorized_keys on every CI runner that pulls them.

findings.title: CVE-2026-31431 Linux Kernel CopyFail — Added to CISA KEV 2026-05-01, 168+ Public PoC Repos, Active Exploitation Confirmed
findings.affected_technology: Linux kernel algif_aead (AF_ALG socket / crypto subsystem); kernels 4.14+ since Aug 2017 across all major distros (Ubuntu, RHEL, SUSE, Amazon Linux, Debian)
findings.summary: CISA added Linux kernel CopyFail LPE to the KEV catalog 2026-05-01 after active in-the-wild exploitation; 168+ public PoC repos in Rust/C/Python/Zig/Go now exist, escalating from 'noted/out-of-scope LPE' to a fleet-wide privilege-escalation pivot for any landed-shell adversary.

findings.title: CVE-2026-42167 ProFTPD mod_sql SQL Injection → Pre-Auth RCE / Auth Bypass / Privilege Escalation
findings.affected_technology: ProFTPD <1.3.9a with mod_sql extension; PostgreSQL-backed FTPS/SFTP appliances; SQLAuthenticate-configured deployments
findings.summary: Logic flaw in ProFTPD mod_sql is_escaped_text() bypasses SQL sanitization via single-quote-bracketed usernames, yielding pre-auth RCE when PostgreSQL DB user is superuser (COPY TO PROGRAM) or auth-bypass under SQLAuthenticate; two public PoCs.

findings.title: Trellix Source Code Repository Breach — 'Portion' of Internal Code Accessed; No Exploitation Evidence Yet
findings.affected_technology: Trellix internal source-code repository (EDR/XDR vendor merging former McAfee Enterprise + FireEye assets)
findings.summary: Trellix confirmed unauthorised access to a portion of its internal source-code repository 2026-05-02 with no current exploitation evidence, but the access has obvious EDR-bypass-development potential — watch the next 30 days for downstream Trellix-process abuse.

noted.product: ConsentFix v3 — Microsoft Azure CLI device-code phishing toolkit refinement (Push Security)
noted.summary: Phishing/OAuth toolkit v3 automates Azure CLI device-code abuse via Pipedream webhooks for real-time refresh-token exchange; Push notes 'unclear if v3 has gained any traction yet' — track for adoption and tune device-code-flow detection.

44 fake GitHub repos spoofing PsExec/AzCopy/Sysmon/LAPS/KustoExplorer redirect to a hidden secondary GitHub account hosting MSI installers carrying EtherRAT (Ethereum-blockchain-resolved C2); targets enterprise admins — block direct GitHub→endpoint admin-tool downloads outside curated artifact stores.

noted.product: FIRESTARTER backdoor — federal Cisco Firepower / ASA campaign (CISA ED 25-03)
noted.summary: CISA Emergency Directive ED 25-03 deadline for federal agencies to inventory all Firepower / Secure Firewall devices was 2026-05-01 23:59 EST (yesterday); FIRESTARTER (LINE VIPER post-exploit) persists on ASA/FTD even after patching CVE-2025-20333 / CVE-2025-20362 — if you operate ASA/Firepower edge devices and have not done a forensic check, do it now.

Linux variant of Harvester APT's GoGra backdoor uses Microsoft Graph API + hard-coded Outlook mailbox folder ('Zomato Pizza') for C2; targets observed in India/Afghanistan via VirusTotal artefacts; ELF disguised as PDF — hunt for Graph API calls from Linux servers without legitimate Microsoft 365 integration.

noted.product: Sentry self-hosted multi-organisation SAML SSO account-takeover
noted.summary: Critical GitHub Security Advisory affecting sentry 21.12.0–26.4.0 self-hosted multi-org deployments published in past 48h (Sentry SaaS already patched 2026-02-18) — patch self-hosted to current and verify SENTRY_SINGLE_ORGANIZATION posture.
Uncontrolled recursion in pyasn1 ≤0.6.2 enables denial of service with sub-100KB input; wide indirect dependency surface (cryptography tooling, LDAP libs) — bump pinned versions.

noted.product: MikroTik RouterOS 6.49.8 SCEP endpoint OOB read
noted.summary: Out-of-bounds read in SCEP endpoint of RouterOS 6.49.8 (CVSS 7.3) with public exploit; niche but check internet-exposed MikroTik fleets for SCEP exposure.

noted.product: ChatGPTNextWeb / NextChat MCP server authorization bypass
noted.summary: Continues the MCP-server-as-attack-surface pattern (mcp-atlassian, nginx-ui, OpenHarness, chatgpt-mcp-server, Branch Monkey MCP, Astro MCP, NextChat); CVSS 7.3 — add to MCP server inventory tracking.
Insecure direct object reference allowing user deletion (CVSS 8.1); requires authenticated vendor-level access but WCFM is a popular multivendor marketplace plugin — patch to latest.

noted.product: npm malware wave (apple-pay-trust/cancelled, apple-internal-security-library-v99, gunpowder-ghost, sirens-lament, blackbeards-navigator)
noted.summary: Multiple typo-/look-alike npm malware packages flagged in GitHub Security Advisories 2026-05-01/02; no distribution numbers but worth a registry-wide scan and lockfile audit.

noted.product: WordPress plugin batch (User Verification, User Registration Advanced Fields, Profile Builder, Export/Import Users, WP Mail Gateway)
noted.summary: Five WordPress registration/auth-adjacent plugin advisories from Positive Technologies dbugs (CVSS 9.8 / 9.8 / 8.1 / 8.8 / 8.8) published 2026-04-30 → 2026-05-02 — high-risk on any membership-style site.
Critical (CVSS 9.0) router vulnerability disclosed 2026-05-02; out of corporate-infra scope but worth flagging if your remote-worker fleet has Trendnet.
Reminder: KEV addition 2026-04-29; if any ScreenConnect installs are still <23.9.8 in your environment you are now non-compliant with the federal remediation deadline.

Ryan Goldberg + Kevin Martin sentenced to 4 years 2026-05-01 for deploying BlackCat against US victims while employed at Sygnia / DigitalMint; useful policy reference for IR-program insider-risk language.
